title: The genocide that still haunts Namibia
content: Between 1904 and 1908 colonial troops in German South West Africa killed tens of thousands of Herero and Nama. Their descendants feel powerless in the face of impending economic upheavals.
snippet: Between 1904 and 1908 colonial troops in German South West Africa killed tens of thousands of Herero and Nama. Their descendants feel powerless in the face of impending economic upheavals.
creator: Clotilde Ravel

title: Indonesia: depoliticised injustice
content: Indonesia's leftwing activists used to believe Reformasi would produce economic policy that was pro organised labour. They're still waiting.
snippet: Indonesia's leftwing activists used to believe Reformasi would produce economic policy that was pro organised labour. They're still waiting.
creator: Vedi R Hadiz

title: Where the Ganges and Brahmaputra meet
content: The Sundarbans Biosphere Reserve is the world's largest mangrove forest. But as rising seas erode the delta, the balance between conservation and human survival is fragile.
snippet: The Sundarbans Biosphere Reserve is the world's largest mangrove forest. But as rising seas erode the delta, the balance between conservation and human survival is fragile.
creator: Bertrand Daugeron

title: Food shocks
content: The agricultural sector's entrenched dependence on fertiliser has exposed it to geopolitical instability. Contemporary struggles over supply are nothing new. The closure over the past months of the Strait of Hormuz, through which passes roughly one third of internationally traded fertiliser, choked off a vital artery of the global food system. Modern agriculture is built on synthetic fertilisers, locking farmers into an input-intensive system driven by competition, profit and state (…)
